name: Armstrong Park
description:
Right next to houses so probably don’t start until 8am, as a courtesy. 
There is one tennis court also.

name: Bear Levin Studer Family YMCA
description: Bear Levin Studer Family YMCA is a place to play pickleball in Pensacola, FL. There are 2 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Reservations are available. Amenities include restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights.

name: Hollis T. Williams Park
description: Come play pickleball at Hollis T. Williams Park in Pensacola, FL! There are 3 outdoor courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free.

name: Navy Federal Credit Union Recreation Center
description: Navy Federal Credit Union Recreation Center is a place to play pickleball in Pensacola, FL. There are 2 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. You can reserve the courts. Players have access to restrooms, water, lights, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Pensacola Athletic Center
description: Pensacola Athletic Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Pensacola, FL. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights. A membership is required to play.

name: Pensacola State College
description: Pensacola State College is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Pensacola, FL. There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Players have access to lights and restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Pensacola Tennis & Pickle
description: Come play pickleball at Pensacola Tennis & Pickle in Pensacola, FL! There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Players have access to restrooms, lights, youth programming, and trainers or lessons. You can reserve the courts.

name: Portside Pickle
description: Come play pickleball at Portside Pickle in Pensacola, FL! There are 3 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, youth programming, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, trainers or lessons, wheelchair accessibility, and adaptive programming. You can reserve the courts.
fees: Fee required — Membership and/or one-time fees apply.

name: Salvation Army Pensacola
description: Salvation Army Pensacola is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Pensacola, FL. There are 4 hard courts. 1 is outdoors and 3 are indoors. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. The courts cannot be reserved. Amenities include restrooms, water, and trainers or lessons. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: SeaSpray Perdido Key Condominiums
description: SeaSpray Perdido Key Condominiums is a place to play pickleball in Pensacola, FL. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are private. You can’t reserve the courts. Amenities include restrooms.

name: UWF Pickleball
description: 3 permanent courts with 4 additional permanent lined courts on back tennis courts. Bring your own net for those back courts.

name: Vickrey Community Center
description: There are limited hours for pickleball play. Tuesday and Thursday evenings only.
